events.title: Build Skills to Support Yourself
events.description: Prepare for moments of anxiety or stress with strategies and resources to calm body and mind. The Center for Women and Community's (CWC’s) Support Skill Building Series: Drop in for 1, 2 or all 3 Small Group Workshops to practice strategies to support yourself, your friends, and your community in times of stress or crisis.

events.title: Edible Book Festival
events.venue: Library, W. E. B. Du Bois
events.description: Edible Book Festival Entry Submission Form Join the Libraries for our ever-popular annual Edible Book Festival, where we make, judge, and eat culinary creations based on literature! You do not need to submit an entry to attend, but if you choose to enter a submission, please complete the above form by Monday, March 30, 2026, 9 a.m. Entries contributed after that will not be eligible for judging. Entries must be brought to the W. E. B. Du Bois Library, RECESS, on Monday, March 31, between 10 a.m. and noon. All visitors to the Library are invited to view the entries from noon to 2 p.m. and cast

events.title: Intern Abroad: A UMass Abroad Alumni Panel
events.description: Double the impact of your education abroad experience by interning while you study abroad! Join UMass Abroad alumni hear about their internships abroad and learn about the internship placement process at CET Academic Programs.

events.title: Chapel Unplugged
events.description: 🎶✨ Chapel Unplugged ✨🎶 Live music, creative crafts, free food, and good vibes. Swing by, hang out, and unplug. March 30th 2026, 4-6:30 pm, in the Old Chapel #HowDoUGrow #NationalNutritionMonth

events.title: Amphibian Week
events.description: It's amphibian week in Morrill 146! Learn about amphibian migrations and enter our contest to win a frog (plushie, of course)! The exhibit is open Monday-Friday, 9:00 am - 5:00 pm.

events.title: Tay Gavin Erickson Lecture Series: Black Data Stories Matter; Dr. Kenton Rambsy
events.description: Tay Gavin Erikson Lecture series Dr. Kenton Rambsy; Howard University Black Data Stories Matter: REGISTER HERE Many African American storytellers have work that contains a multitude of reference points, which scholars have long examined through close reading and historical interpretation. Yet, new questions emerge when we also examine the numerical patterns, circulation histories, and cultural data that shape how Black writers are read, taught, and remembered. In this presentation, Dr. Kenton Rambsy explains how data storytelling can expand the study of Black writers and other topics across Bl

events.title: Build Skills to Emotionally Support Yourself
events.description: Prepare for moments of anxiety or stress with strategies and resources to calm body and mind. CWC’s Support Skill Building Series: Drop in for 1, 2 or all 3 Small Group Workshops to practice strategies to support yourself, your friends, and your community in times of stress or crisis.

events.title: ASK THE ATTORNEY SERIES
events.description: Ask the Attorney DROP-IN Hour for INTERNATIONAL STUDENTS Do you have immigration related questions? Join us for FREE, confidential legal consulation with SLSO Attorney Ben Farrell. Tuesday, February 10, 2026 2:00 to 3:00 PMTuesday, March 10, 2026 2:00 to 3:00 PMTuesday, March 31, 2026 2:00 to 3:00 PMTuesday, April 14, 2026 2:00 to 3:00 PMTuesday, April 28, 2026 2:00 to 3:00 PM

events.title: All About Patents
events.venue: Library, W. E. B. Du Bois
events.description: Have an idea for a new invention and want to see if it's been done before? Interested in learning how to find the latest technology major companies are exploring? Want to just learn more about what patents are and how to find them? Join the University of Massachusetts Libraries and Western Massachusetts Patent and Trademark Resource Center for an introduction to patents and patent searching.

events.title: Trans Day of Self-Care
events.description: For the Trans Day of Visibility, we are holding a Trans Day of Self-Care. There will be therapy dogs, a massage therapist, cupcakes from Sweets & More, and crafts. Sponsored by the Stonewall Center and Sweets & More.

events.title: 2026 Troy Lecture - N.K. Jemisin
events.description: Department of English Presents 2026 Troy Lecture with N. K. Jemisin UMass Amherst’s Department of English announces the 2026 Troy Lecture with N. K. Jemisin, one of the most acclaimed writers of speculative fiction and fantasy of the twenty-first century. The event will take place on Tuesday, March 31 at 4:30pm in the Student Union Ballroom at UMass Amherst. The event is free and open to the public; tickets – available through the UMass Amherst Fine Arts Center – are required for entry. N. K. Jemisin is a fantasy author and 2020 MacArthur Fellow. Since beginning her professional career in the

events.title: Photography As Storytelling: Presentation and Conversation
events.description: Presented in partnership by the University Without Walls Department of Interdisciplinary Studies and the UMass Amherst Henry M. Thomas III Center in Springfield as part of a Community Conversation and Workshop Series exploring the theme “In These Times: Documenting Stories Through Our Own Lenses,” we examine the relationship between power, narrative and the importance of preserving community and individual stories for future generations. This Presentation and Conversation will focus on Photography As Storytelling. Featuring Panelists- Aiko Wakao Austin, a Japanese interdisciplinary artist expl

events.title: Talking Tech: How to Use Chatbots Safely
events.description: AI chatbots are becoming our new coworkers. Can we trust them? In the second installment of our Talking Tech series, join UMass faculty Francine Berman and Laura Haas for discussion about what chatbots can do, what they can’t do, and where the limits should be. Sponsored by Manning College of Information and Computer Sciences and Public Interest Technology Initiative at UMass, Talking Tech aims to demystify the technology that drives our world. Francine Berman is Director of Public Interest Technology in the Manning College of Computer and Information Sciences at UMass, as well as a Faculty As

events.title: BPQC Meeting: Bi & Trans Intersections
events.description: The Bi/Pan/Queer Collective (BPQC), a social group for bi, pan, queer, and sexually fluid students, will be discussing bi & trans intersections at its next meeting to commemorate the Trans Day of Visibility. Open to all students.

events.title: Libraries' Page to Screen Club
events.description: Drop in to the Libraries' multimedia book club, where we read a book and then watch the screen adaptation! We meet for several weeks to talk about the book, then watch the screen adaptation together and compare. All sessions are 7-8:30 p.m. except for screenings, which are 7-9:30 p.m. The breakdown for what pages/sections to read will be determined at a later planning session. Little Women, Louisa May Alcott February 3 February 10 February 17 February 24 March 3 - Screening Stories of Your Life and Others, Ted Chiang March 10 March 17 - No meeting; spring vacation March 24 - Screening Jurassic
